mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, validating corporate revenue forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, which triggers short-term positive equity repricing as investors anticipate higher near-term earnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered periods during recovery phases, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising borrowing costs and compressing equity multiples, which generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative direction in >50% of periods with payroll growth, or rolling Rank IC drops below 0.01 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and retail sales signals synchronized strength in durable goods and consumer discretionary demand, producing positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Combined positive signals yield negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coinciding with elevated inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks and cost-push pressures, triggering negative short-term equity returns as investors price in stagflation risks.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance during supply-constrained periods.
